name: Marions Best Ever Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 - 3 oz Each of cold butter and cold lard
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the pastry, cut the butter and lard into the flour using a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
2. Scatter the ice cold water over the mixture, mixing with a fork until a dough forms a ball.
3. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and chill for about 30 minutes.
4. Divide the dough into two balls.
5. Roll out one portion to fit a 9-inch pie pan, leaving 1 inch of pastry at the edge.
6. Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C).
7. Core, peel, and thinly slice the apples. Toss with cinnamon, sugar, and lemon juice in a bowl.
8. Sprinkle bread crumbs evenly over the pie shell.
9. Arrange the apple slices on top, leaving as little space between slices as possible. Pile the slices high but do not mound in the center.
10. Roll out the other half of dough to fit the top of the pie.
11. Brush the edge of the bottom crust with egg wash made by blending the beaten egg yolk with milk.
12. Place the top layer of dough on the pie, tucking it under the bottom layer of dough around the edge of the pie.
13. Crimp the edges with fingers to form fluting.
14. Prick the pie in at least a dozen places with a fork to allow steam to escape.
15. Brush the pie with egg wash.
16. Bake for 15 minutes.
17. Reduce the oven temperature to 400°F (200°C) and bake for an additional 15 minutes.
18. Reduce the temperature to 325°F (160°C) and bake for another 20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
